page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, providing a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. No public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i-flex.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in multi-core and compute-intensive tasks. It achieved a Passmark CPU score of 83,206.18 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 20,959.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The hardware excels in static web serving, reaching a throughput of 104.85 GB/sec, and shows strong capabilities in medium-model LLM inference, processing 303.91 tokens per second for 7B model prompts. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the flex-class hardware profile offers high compute density and advanced instruction sets, though the lack of local NVMe storage and the 2 GB RAM per vCPU limit represent clear tradeoffs for memory-bound or storage-heavy workloads.

This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including high-performance web serving, software compilation, image processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference. It is also well-suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks and high-throughput database operations that do not require local SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, but lacks local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier performance, scoring 83,206.18 on Passmark CPU and achieving 104.85 GB/sec in static web serving. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference and software compilation. This instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, including web serving, image processing, and multi-threaded data processing, offering high resource density at a competitive qualitative cost-to-performance ratio for CPU-bound tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU.
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for vector and matrix acceleration.
Delivers top-tier multi-core performance and 104.85 GB/sec static web serving throughput.
No local storage included, relying entirely on network-attached storage.
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
